methods.description: Processing sediment samples using standard techniques including chemical treatments (sodium hexametaphosphate, HCl, HF) and sieving to isolate the palynomorph fraction, followed by microscopic identification and counting of various palynomorph types (pollen, spores, fungal remains, foraminifer test linings, dinoflagellate cysts) to determine their content, concentrations, and percentages.

steps.description: sediments of approximately ∼ 5 cm 2 volume/∼ 8 g dry weight were processed for palynological analysis
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment samples for palynological processing by measuring volume or dry weight.

steps.description: including treatment with warm, diluted (0.02 %) sodium hexametaphosphate
steps.searchable_summary: Treat sediment samples with warm, diluted sodium hexametaphosphate.
Treat sediment samples with weak HCl.
Treat sediment samples with concentrated HF.
outputs: Sediment samples after HF treatment (palynomorph residue)

steps.description: and sieving through Nitex mesh to retain the > 15 µm fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve the processed sediment residue through a Nitex mesh to retain particles larger than 15 µm.

steps.description: Lycopodium spore tablets were added in order to calculate pollen concentrations (Stockmarr, 1971).
steps.searchable_summary: Add Lycopodium spore tablets to the palynomorph fraction as a marker for concentration calculation.

steps.description: Palynomorphs were identified at 400×/500×/1000× magnification using a Zeiss Axio Scope.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify palynomorph types using microscopy at specified magnifications.

steps.description: Sixty-one samples from Site M0027 were analyzed for their palynomorph content. For most samples, between 200 and 300 non-saccate pollen grains (NSPGs) and between 250 and 400 pteridophyte spores, non-saccate pollen, and saccate pollen grains (in total) have been counted. In case of three samples, the pollen sum was between 140 and 200 grains. In addition to pollen grains and pteridophyte spores, fungal remains, foraminifer test linings (forams), and dinoflagellate cysts (dinocysts) were counted.
steps.searchable_summary: Count the number of different palynomorph types (NSPGs, spores, saccate pollen, fungal remains, forams, dinocysts) in each sample.
outputs: Raw counts of different palynomorph types per sample

steps.description: Lycopodium spore tablets were added in order to calculate pollen concentrations (Stockmarr, 1971).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate palynomorph concentrations using the known quantity of added Lycopodium spores and their count relative to the palynomorph counts.
inputs: Raw counts of palynomorphs and Lycopodium spores
Known quantity of added Lycopodium spores

steps.description: We used the total number of NSPGs as reference sums for all percentage calculations (Figs. 2 and 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the percentage of each palynomorph type relative to the total number of non-saccate pollen grains (NSPGs).
inputs: Raw counts of different palynomorph types
Total count of NSPGs per sample
outputs: Palynomorph percentages relative to NSPGs per sample

steps.description: The data used for this study will be stored in the PANGEA database (www.pangea.de).
steps.searchable_summary: Store the processed palynological data (counts, concentrations, percentages) in the PANGEA database.
